### [0:00 - 1:24]
**英文原文**:
So, I be the most integral of them all. And as always, I'm thinking about ratio. Oh, boy. Well, you know, there's not enough leaders at the socials. The ratio was terrible. I don't like the ratio. And I'll be like, "Oh my god, you. Where are the leaders?" And then, I started walking around and there was a lounge area. And then, they were sitting, sleeping, lounging, just talking with people. Yeah, I mean, like, think about leaders there as cooks or chefs with the same amount of ingredients to make 30 meals in one night. You see that it is a wicked problem. Lots of movie parts, high complexity, some competing elements, right? Which is something really hard. Followers feel more pressure to do well and to execute everything to perfection on the later stages, where the leaders feel more pressure to do everything super correctly and comfortably at the early stages. >> Early stages. One of the reasons why sometimes followers come for private lessons to learn more is very simply, "Oh, I want to get invited to this more often." What if you try a little less hard? Hello, hello, bom dia, bom dia, everyone. And welcome back to another episode. My name is Alison Sandee and our amazing co-host, Gui Brada. Hello, everybody. Welcome back.

**中文對照**:
所以，我是其中最不可或缺的一位。就像往常一樣，我總是考慮比例這個問題。哦，天啊。你知道的，在舞會上領導者的比例真的很糟糕。我不喜歡這種比例。我總會問：「天哪，你們這是什麼情況？領導者呢？」然後我開始走動，看到了休息區，有人正坐在那裡、打瞌睡或是閒聊。是的，想想看，把領導者比作廚師吧，他們有相同的食材要在一個晚上做出三十頓飯。你會發現這是一個很複雜的問題，像電影中的多條線索相互交織，許多競爭元素，對吧？這真的非常難處理。跟隨者的壓力在後期更大，需要做得更完美、更沒有瑕疵；而領導者則是在前期就需要表現得非常正確且自在。>> 前期。這是為什麼有些跟隨者會來上私人課堂，只為更多次被邀請的一個簡單理由：「我想多參加幾場舞會。」如果你稍微放輕鬆點會怎樣呢？嗨、嗨，早上好、大家好，歡迎回到新的一集Podcast。我是艾莉森．桑迪，與我們一同主持的還有 Gui 布拉達，各位早上好，歡迎回歸。

So, Gui, what is the question for this episode? So, I be the most integral of them all. Yeah? It's a question from Billy Lou. From last year, December 16th, where it says that he's doing a leadership course and he was introduced to the concept of a wicked problem, which is a problem that is difficult or impossible to solve because of incomplete, contradictory, and changing requirements that are often difficult to recognize. High complexity, lots of moving parts, probably some competing elements, lack of transparency, and so on. So, what would people say are the biggest wicked problems in the zouk scene right now? Why are they wicked problems? >> [laughter] >> Where do we start? Hey, well, I don't know. Do you want to start? >> Yes, let's go and I can think of one at the top of my head, which I'm already thinking about on a daily basis because I'm the organizer for BraZouki. And as of now, in just over 2 weeks, we'll be

**中文對照**:
好，在這集Podcast中，我們會繼續進行實驗，閱讀我們Facebook Zouk Nerds團體中的幾個問題和留言，並像一次Q&A初階對話一樣回答。面對面地分享經驗，或許能幫助到你。所以Gui，這個節目的問題是什麼？

我就是其中最核心的一個。是Billy Lou去年12月16日提出來的問題。他參加了一場領導力課程，學到了一個稱為「惡劣問題」的概念——這是一種因為不完整、矛盾和改變的要求而難以解決，甚至可能是不可能解決的問題。這些要求往往難以辨識，複雜度高，涉及許多因素，還可能有相互競爭的因素，缺乏透明度等等。那麼，在Zouk舞場景中，人們認為最大的惡劣問題是什麼？為什麼它是惡劣問題？

>> [笑聲] >>
我們該從哪個地方開始呢？嘿，我不知道，你想先說嗎？
>> 好吧，我們就來開始吧，我有一個在我每天思考的問題。我是BraZouki的 organizer，再有兩個多星期，我們就要舉辦活動了。

### [2:59 - 4:24]
**英文原文**:
having BraZouki in Australia. And as always, I'm thinking about ratio. Oh, boy. BraZouki is a worldwide festival that we always, since day one, control the ratio very, very strictly. But, even with controlling the passes and at some years having a as close as possible to one-to-one ratio, I do have like a formula there that shows me 1.1, 1.2, 1.something. When it comes to the social, especially the social, what do we do? Okay, so what happens usually is in the beginning of the night, we have lots of leaders, not enough follows. And then in the middle of the night you have the social, we have I would say a good ratio of both and everybody's happy. But then when it comes to like maybe the third to the fourth quarter, Mhm. Leaders gone. Followers still there. And then the problem comes with the

**中文對照**:
在澳洲舉辦 BraZouki。就像往常一樣，我一直考慮著比例問題。Oh, boy。BraZouki 是一個全球性的舞蹈節，從頭開始，我們總是嚴格控制參與的比例。我們不提供夜間票或日間票這種東西。如果你想要完整的體驗，那就親自來參加吧。當然，當需要其他票種平衡時，我們也會保留一些票券。但是，即使在控制票券數量的情況下，有些年份也盡可能保持接近一對一的比例，我还是有一個公式，顯示著1.1、1.2或某種程度的1.3。到了社交舞的時候，特別是社交舞的時間段，我們該怎麼做呢？通常來說，在晚上開始時，領舞的人很多，但隨後就不夠跟舞者了。而到了半夜，社交舞的比例就會變得比較均衡，大家都開心。但是當進入第三或第四個四分之一時間點時，Mhm，領舞的人少了，跟舞者還在。接著問題就出現了。

### [4:21 - 5:41]
**英文原文**:
follows who came late to the party. They came to the second half of the party and they see everybody busy, everybody going, but then more and more and more they see less leaders to dance with. And then of course they share the feedback with us and tell us, "Well, you know, there's not enough leaders at the socials. There's not enough leaders. The ratio was terrible. I don't like the ratio." And then we're like, "I did I did my best." We cannot control who shows up at the social, at what time they show up at the social. So, well, if I can share my feedback and my suggestion is especially for socials where you find that there is this problem, come early. Come early. At District Zoo in DC and in many other communities that I've been visiting or helped being part of, I noticed this. This is sort of a pattern where [clears throat] leaders show up earlier. At the start of the social you have more leaders. Yes. We do have you know, we have discussed about these and we do have our theories about it. Most of the times we agreed that leaders want to come early because they want to

**中文對照**:
他們晚來參加舞會，等到了後半場，看到大家都在忙碌、翩翩起舞，卻越來越難找到舞伴。於是他們向我們反饋：「社交舞會上領舞者太少，比例太差了，我不滿意這個比例。」我們則回答：「我已經盡力了。」我們無法控制參加社交舞會的人什麼時候到場，所以建議大家還是早點來吧。在DC的District Zoo以及其他許多社區中，我都注意到這種現象。領舞者通常比較早到場，在舞會開始時就有更多領舞者在場。關於這個問題，我們確實討論過，也提出了一些理論，大多數時候我們認為領舞者選擇早到的原因是他們希望早早佔據舞池。

### [5:38 - 7:00]
**英文原文**:
get warmed up, they want to practice their moves before the social really kicks in and everybody arrives. So, they have that interest of being there early. But, they're there early and they dance non-stop until, let's say, the middle of the social. And most of the times, those are the same leaders that have already done a ton of classes on that same day. So, let's say by 12:00 a.m., 1:00 a.m., they are dead. Body, mind, soul, feet, joints, muscles, everything. Now, on the other side, you have the follows who will be showing up a little later, usually, and when they show up, they already don't have as many leaders to dance with. And because of that, they take longer breaks, not because they want to, just because of how the social is working. So, they don't get as tired as early as the leaders. Which means they will stay until 3:00 or 4:00 a.m., but they're not going to get as many dances. So, how to solve this problem? I still don't know. But, all I can do is to try and make sure that at least at sign up, through the door, we have the same amount of leads and follows.

**中文對照**:
暖身後，他們希望在正式舞會開始前先練習幾個動作。因此，他們喜歡早點到場。但這些人一早就來了，從頭到尾都在跳舞，直到午夜或凌晨一點鐘才停下來。大多數時候，這些領舞者已經上了很多堂課。到了凌晨十二點或一點鐘，他們累得不行，身體、心智、靈魂都透支了。

另一方面，跟跳的人通常較晚到場，當他們來的時候，可選擇的領舞者不多，因此他們需要更長時間休息。這並不是因為他們想這樣做，而是因為這種情況自然造成。所以跟跳者的疲勞程度相對低一些。這意味著他們可能會待到凌晨三點或四點鐘，但得到的跳舞機會卻沒有那麼多。

那麼要如何解決這個問題呢？我還不知道該怎麼做，但我至少能在報名時確保進門時男女比例相同。

---

And to be honest, I do have other tricks up my sleeve when it comes to festivals. While I was there I was also in my observation mode and it was really interesting cuz like you were saying that even amounts even with taxi dancers I think it was me and two other leaders

**中文對照**:
我們 masih 有一群舞者在等候領舞。當我問他們在哪裡時，他們正在休息區聊天或小憩。這確實很有趣。如果我們現在考慮的話，其實我們可以舉辦一次領舞高峰會，讓大家都坐下來聊聊，這樣我們才能更好地了解原因。這是棘手的問題所在，因為這種情況在大多數社交舞環境中都普遍存在。我們這裡不是在谈论初學者，他們確實比較難以應對。我們是在談論有經驗的舞者。是的，我們是指150位領舞、150位跟隨者以及更多的領舞高手，他們仍然比跟隨者更加連接社交舞環境。確實，大多數時候，初學者不會參加這樣的活動。當然，對他們來說這可能會太多了。所以說，你報名參加這樣的活動時，已經在社區投入很多了。你已經參加過不少社交舞會，認識了不少人。

### [9:41 - 11:06]

how the system or the engine and the society or social dancers work. And the interesting thing, too, is why is that a wicked problem? Because it's also a wicked problem at the scene's leadership level, at the scene's management level. Mhm. Guys, we're talking about in general here. Yeah. Cuz I know there are these very, like, particular cases of scenes that we do have more leaders than followers. We are not pointing any fingers here. They exist. I know. I've been there. I've lived in there. And I have had the experience of sitting down in a social and nobody comes to invite me to dance because they're all dancing. Magical days. Magical moments. And that I could sit down and watch. At the leadership level of a dance community we also do have a majority being followers. A majority being women, as well. And something that is really interesting, even in the District Zouk court scene we needed to you know, shut the gates for new primary followers to join the court scene because we are at a situation that we have like 18 people and three of them are leaders.

**中文對照**:
SOCIAL舞或音樂文化的運作機制。有趣的是，這為什麼是一個棘手的問題？因為在舞蹈社群的領導層和管理層面上也是一樣。我們這裡是泛指情況。的确，在某些場合中， Leader比Follower多。我們並沒有指責任何人。確實存在這種情況，我也曾在那裡度過時期，親身經歷過在SOCIAL舞會中坐著看眾人跳舞的時刻，那真是一種魔幻的體驗。而在舞蹈社群的領導層面，Follower也佔了大多數，其中女性更是占了絕大部分。即使是在District Zouk舞場場合中，我們也曾不得不限制新Follower加入，因為當時已有18位舞者，而 Leader只有三位。

**英文原文**:
And more people want to come in and they're all followers. So, the people who want to become assistants, who want to become teachers, the people who want to be in some shape or form participating in the growth of their community are followers, too. To be clear, the core team of our school that we're talking about, the assistants, the teachers, the trainees, the DJs, the admin, everybody who make the whole machine work, we call that the core team. Core team, yeah. And when I think about other scenes as well, the majority of them are followers. I can think about three scenes right now who did two main teachers are two primary followers where one of them is learning how to lead. So, you know, they can have the leading follow situation. And I actually would even say, I don't know if this is related, but I personally, me myself and I and everybody in my head loves those communities because they're usually the best ones run. **中文對照**:
更多的人想加入，他們都是跟隨者。想要成為助理、教師，或以各種形式參與社群成長的人也是跟隨者。請注意，我們所說的學校核心團隊包括助理、教師、學員、DJ和行政人員，所有人共同構成了整個機制，我們稱之為核心團隊。核心团队就是核心团队。

當我想到其他舞場時，大多數也都是由跟隨者構成。目前我能想到三個舞場，兩位主要老師中有一位是主領的新手跟隨者。所以他們可以有帶領跟隨的狀況。實際上，我自己和周圍的人都很喜歡這些社群，因為通常是由跟隨者們經營得最好。

附帶一提，這點我每次想到某個城市時都會回想起：“哦，對。哦，對。”你只需看看我的日曆就知道了。

---

### [12:15 - 13:37]
**英文原文**:
Pretty much all the communities that I travel and work with, they're run by followers. Yeah. And I think we have discussed and everybody and their mothers and their family have discussed why we don't have enough leaders in general in the local scenes in the Brazilian Zouk, you know, global scene, exception of some of these places that we saw here in our comments where the leader passes sell fast. And this is something really interesting because when I think about, for example, the entry bar, for example, the moment in which a dancer feels comfortable to attend a social or in a more, you know, at a later stage, feel comfortable to attend a full-on event. It's very hard for a beginner leader to buy a pass to a full-on event unless they are embedded with this courage or with this, "Man, I'm just going to go enjoy life." Most people are feeling secure. Most people think that their skill sets are not developed enough for them to go out and social dance for 4 days, you know, and take classes and stuff. So, it's really important to understand, generally speaking, that it's more

**中文對照**:
基本上，我所接觸的各個社群都是由跟隨者掌管。是的，我們確實討論過為什麼在Brazilian Zouk 舞舞的地方社群中 leader 的人數普遍不足，全球範圍內亦然，除了某些地方例外，例如我們評論中提到的一些地點，leader 的票很快就賣光了。這是一項非常有趣的觀察，因為當我思考參加社交舞會或大型活動的門檻時，比如一個舞者感到自在到足以參加社交舞會，或者更晚些時候，有信心參加完整的大型活動，對初學者來說要買全票進入這些活動是非常困難的。初學leader很少能夠單純因為興趣和勇氣就購買這樣的全票。大多數人感到安全，認為自己的技術尚不夠成熟，不足以連續四天去參加社交舞會、上課等等。因此，理解這一點很重要——就是說，在一般情況下，要達到這個階段是需要更多努力的。

---

### [13:35 - 14:59]
**英文原文**:
likely for a beginner follower to go to socials, to go to big events from like early stages in their social dance life, than it is for beginner leader. And I do think that at times the beginner leader, or even the more advanced leader, depending on how you look at it, might also get tired quicker, maybe, because of the amount of things that they are managing. It's almost like this silent process that is happening in the back. Music, space, floor craft, leads, this, all these things are going on. And it might be that the person might get tired quicker, or might get overwhelmed quicker. And tired quicker, not necessarily physically tired, but more mentally tired, because a leader is is stuck with their own movements and their own dance over and over again. The follower gets to experience more movements, more variety in terms of vocabulary, more often than the leaders are. So, there is this aspect, I think, of mentally tired, or mentally exhausted, that might happen. And that's the thing that I think happened in the marathon that I was there where I have even passes.

**中文對照**:
對於初學跟跳者的來說，參加社交舞會或大型活動可能比初學 Leads 更常見。我認為，在某些時候，即使是進階的 Leads，從不同角度看，也可能更快感到疲倦，因為他們需要管理的事情更多。這就像背後有一個默默進行的過程：音樂、空間利用、 Floor Craft（ Flooring 技術）、 Leaders 的引導等，都在同時發生。跟跳者可能會更快感到疲倦或被壓垮，這種疲倦並非一定是身體上的，而是更可能是心理上的。因為 Leads 一次又一次地反覆著自己的動作和舞步，而跟跳者則可以體驗到更多樣的動作和舞步。因此，在心理上感到疲倦甚至精疲力盡的情況是有可能發生的。這正是我在那場馬拉松活動中所經歷的事情。

---

### [14:57 - 16:22]
**英文原文**:
Still had long line of followers on the floor, and a bunch of leaders sitting down sleeping, chatting, hanging out, you know. So, I think it might be something like that. Yeah, I mean, like, think about leaders that are as cooks or chefs with the same amount of ingredients to make 30 meals in one night. Yes. You know? So, they only have the same amount of ingredients and maybe techniques and equipments in the kitchen. But they need to make 30 plates. They can choose to make the same menu, the same offering for all the 30 plates, or they can choose to be creative and multiply and do something. But it gets really tiring. Imagine that, you know, you're either feeling bored with your dancing, which is a problem if you make 30 of the same plate, or you feel really, really exhausted and tired very quickly because you're trying to make a new excellent plate every single time. Yeah. And adding up to the wickedness, to the wicked aspect of this, is thinking about class. Well, sometimes we are in a class and we have two leaders for every follow. And then you go to social, you have

**中文對照**:
Floor 上仍排著長龍般的跟隨者，而許多領舞則坐在一旁打瞌睡、聊天或閒晃。所以我想可能就是這樣的狀況。換句話說，就像廚師要以同樣的食材在一晚做出三十道料理。是的，你懂我意思嗎？所以這些廚師有著相同的食材和技法及工具，但卻需要完成三十道菜。他們可以選擇做同款料理，或者發揮創意、靈活多變地去製作。但這樣會非常疲累。想像一下，如果你每晚都在跳同樣的舞步，可能會覺得乏味，這是個問題；或者你可能很快便精疲力盡，因為每次都要努力做出優異的新舞步。而更糟糕的是，還得考慮到班級制衡。有些課堂中，每個跟隨者都配對了兩位領舞。然後到了社交舞會，情况就不同了。

---

### [16:20 - 17:38]
**英文原文**:
three follows for every leader. But in class there is a lot of leaders, you know? And then but still so I think the leaders might need a safer environment for a longer period of time until they develop the confidence. And then there is this other very interesting thing as well. At times, the classes end up being more leader focused. Man, they end up becoming more leader centric because it's natural for us to have a class that we look more to the leader and then another one that we look more to the follower. And then at times, subconsciously, because we see how fragile presence of the leader is in that situation, we give more attention to the leader. And even in that situation, the followers still are by default, in general, more consistent in class. They keep coming. And they come, and they come again, and they keep coming. And the leaders, even with the force, even with the leader-centric approach at times, they are still more free. You know, you see that it's a classic It is a wicked problem. Lots of moving parts, high complexity, some competing

**中文對照**:
每位領舞者三位跟舞者。但在課堂上，領舞者的數量很多，你知道吧？因此我认為領舞者需要一個更安全的環境較長時間練習，直到他們建立起信心為止。還有一件非常有趣的事。有的時候，課堂反而變得更加以領舞者為中心。天啊，它們變得更傾向於聚焦領舞者，因為我們自然會在課堂上更多關注領舞者，而另一些則是更多关注跟舞者。有些时候，潛意識中，由於領舞者的存在看似脆弱，我們便更加注重新手的表現。即便如此，在這種情況下，跟舞者仍然比較穩定，他們經常來參加課程。他們不斷地來，再来一次，反覆練習。而領舞者即使在強調以領舞者為中心的方法時，仍然較為自由。你知道，這是一個典型的複雜問題。有很多動態因素，高層次的複雜性，還有競爭對立的因素。

### [17:36 - 19:01]
**英文原文**:
elements, right? Which is something really hard. And then there's more war. The cultural thing. Mhm. We are in Asia, where the ratio problem is really intense. Yeah, depending on the country, it can multiply. Multiply by 10, by 100. You know, some countries and some regions, not so much. But yes. Right. >> Or sure. Exactly. I was at an event in Japan, and there was easily double-digits number of followers more than leaders at a social or at an event. So, there's the cultural aspect to it. And then we are now associating a little bit more the role with the gender, where normally the leaders are guys and the followers are women. Where it's easier for a woman to come to social dance than it is for a guy. Culturally speaking, depending on, you know, where do you from. Mhm. You know. So, all these high complex elements combine together to create this as old as time issue, which is the presence of leaders. You know, when I was at Jaime Arocha in Botafogo, huge I needed why the bolsista existed. Why I was the

**中文對照**:
元素啊？這真的非常困難。還有更多的戰爭，文化上的挑戰。嗯，我們在亞洲，比例問題特別嚴重。對，取決於國家，可能會倍數增加。有的國家和地區則不是這樣。是的。對。或者當然了。確實如此。我在日本參加了一個活動，在一次社交舞會或是活動中，跟隨者的人數比領導者的多好幾倍。所以這涉及到文化因素。現在我們也稍微把角色與性別聯繫起來，通常領導者是男性，而跟隨者則是女性。對於女性來說在社交舞會上更容易有所表現，而不像男性這樣。從文化的角度來看，取決於你來自哪裡。嗯，所以這些複雜的因素結合在一起，創造了一個古老的問題——領導者的存在。你知道，在我參加Jaime Arocha舞蹈節時，我很想知道Bolsista的存在原因以及我的角色為什麼是這樣的。

### [18:57 - 20:25]
**英文原文**:
bolsista 151. My number, I was the 151st bolsista of the school out of a total team of around 300 people, where maybe 50 of them were followers. All the rest were leaders. Both sisters are like the helpers, kind of trainees, interns that are there for the classes and some of them, of course, training up to be a teacher. This the same way I started as both sister. If you are a leader or back then as long as you were enter the school and you say, "I want to lead." That's it. You got a position on that day. Immediate. Yeah, that was my case. That day I came in to see how the program worked. And Jaime asked, "Are you ready?" And then we got into the room right away. And there was I was thrown into the jungle right away. And there were classes that we had I can you not 30 something followers and two leaders. You know, all the other followers, both sisters were there doing the class with them. So, even in Brazil even in Rio there we have an abundance of people social dancing we had a problem. And it's not a cultural problem to dance in Brazil. I mean, at least not in Rio. Some states maybe. I can't precise might

**中文對照**:
我當時是學校第151位bolsista，在一支約300人的團隊中，可能只有50人是跟隨者，其餘都是領舞。兩姐妹像是助教或實習生，負責參加課堂教學，有些甚至訓練成為老師。當時我也像兩姐妹一樣開始學習。只要你願意領舞，當時只要進入學校說出你想領舞，當天立刻就能獲得一個位置。這就是我的經歷。那天我來了學校，想看看這個計劃是如何運作的。Jaime問我：「你準備好了嗎？」接著我們馬上進教室了。我被直接扔進了這個環境。當時課堂上有30多個跟隨者和兩個領舞，其他跟隨者包括兩姐妹在內都在進行課堂教學。即使是在巴西裡約熱內盧這樣社交舞如此繁盛的城市，也遇到了一個問題。這並不是文化上的問題，至少在裡約地區不是這樣。其他一些州可能有不同，但我不太清楚。

---
